skill.name: debugging-strategies
skill.description: Apply systematic debugging, profiling, and root cause analysis across codebases, runtime errors, production incidents, and performance issues.
skill.summary: Use structured debugging playbooks to reproduce, isolate, investigate, and verify software defects.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported command execution, dynamic import, network, reconnaissance, and weak cryptography patterns. Review found these alerts are Markdown fences, documentation examples, localhost profiling guidance, or plain text, not executable malicious behavior. Low risk remains because debugging workflows can collect sensitive logs, environment details, heap snapshots, or production data if users apply them carelessly.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. They point to Markdown code fences, inline resource paths, or illustrative debugging commands such as git bisect, not skill code that executes shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens are inside Markdown or language examples. No file contains executable hook code that would run these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: Go Import Examples Misclassified as Dynamic Imports
low_findings.description: The dynamic import alerts are false positives. The flagged lines show Go import blocks for debugging and profiling examples, not JavaScript import expressions or runtime code loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is clearly Go sample code in a Markdown playbook. There is no dynamic JavaScript import or executable loader.

low_findings.title: Localhost Profiling URL Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The network alert is a false positive. The URL is a localhost pprof endpoint shown as documentation for local profiling, not an external network destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only URL uses localhost and appears in a profiling comment. No evidence found of outbound requests, data transfer, or remote exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives. The flagged lines contain prose, error checklist text, or a TypeScript tracing decorator, not cryptographic algorithms or hash functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak crypto usage at the flagged locations. The matches come from unrelated debugging text.

low_findings.title: Debugging Artifacts Can Expose Sensitive Data
low_findings.description: The skill encourages collecting logs, traces, environment details, heap snapshots, and anonymized production data. This is legitimate debugging guidance, but users should avoid exposing secrets or personal data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data collection guidance is explicit and useful for debugging. The risk depends on user handling of logs, environment details, heap dumps, and production data.

content.value_statement: Complex bugs waste time when investigation is unstructured. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a repeatable debugging process.
actual_capabilities: Guides reproduction, evidence collection, hypothesis testing, and fix verification.
Provides checklists for error messages, environments, recent changes, and affected scope.
Explains debugger, logging, profiling, and tracing techniques across common languages.
Suggests binary search, differential debugging, and git bisect workflows.
Covers intermittent bugs, performance issues, and production incident investigation.
limitations: It does not execute debuggers, profilers, or shell commands by itself.
It requires access to relevant logs, traces, errors, or reproduction steps.
It cannot guarantee a root cause without enough observable evidence.
Users must protect secrets and personal data in diagnostic artifacts.

use_cases.title: Investigate a Regression
use_cases.description: Use structured reproduction steps and git history checks to narrow the commit or change that introduced a bug.

use_cases.title: Profile Slow Code
use_cases.description: Select a profiling method, collect timing evidence, and identify the bottleneck before changing implementation details.

use_cases.title: Triage a Production Incident
use_cases.description: Gather logs, metrics, user reports, and safe reproduction data before forming and testing incident hypotheses.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Bug Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me debug this issue systematically. Ask for the symptom, expected behavior, reproduction steps, errors, and recent changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Reproduction Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a minimal reproduction plan for this bug. Include required inputs, environment details, logs to capture, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Form and Test Hypotheses
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these observations, list likely root causes. Design controlled experiments that can prove or disprove each hypothesis.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Production Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide a safe production debugging workflow. Include evidence collection, privacy safeguards, rollback checks, instrumentation, and fix verification.

output_examples.input: A checkout flow fails only for some Safari users after the latest release.
output: Separate browser-specific, release-specific, and user-data hypotheses.
Capture exact Safari versions, request identifiers, console errors, and affected account traits.
Compare the latest release with the previous working release before changing code.

output_examples.input: An API endpoint became slow after a database migration.
output: Measure latency by layer before optimizing.
Compare query plans and row counts before and after the migration.
Verify the fix with the same request shape and dataset size.

output_examples.input: A service crashes after several hours with no clear stack trace.
output: Track memory growth, open handles, request volume, and recent deployments.
Collect safe diagnostic snapshots without exposing secrets.
Test leak and resource exhaustion hypotheses in a staging environment.

best_practices: Reproduce the issue before changing code.
Change one variable at a time and record each result.
Remove or redact sensitive diagnostic data before sharing it.
anti_patterns: Making several changes before testing the result.
Copying production data or heap dumps without privacy controls.

faq.question: What kinds of bugs does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports runtime errors, regressions, intermittent failures, performance issues, production incidents, and unclear application behavior.

faq.question: Does the skill run debugging commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users decide which commands or tools to run.

faq.question: Can it help when I cannot reproduce the issue?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help collect evidence and design reproduction attempts, but a verified fix may need a reproducible signal.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written as general debugging guidance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the symptom, expected behavior, error messages, recent changes, environment details, and steps already tried.

faq.question: How should sensitive logs be handled?
faq.answer: Redact tokens, personal data, credentials, and private production data before sharing logs or diagnostic files.
